Field Label: Record ID

Section Header:
IL Childcare and Children's Health Study

 

Principal Investigator Name and Title:  Sheena Martenies, Assistant Professor

Department and Institution: Health and Kinesiology, University of Illinois

Contact Information: smarte4@illinois.edu | 217-300-9375

Sponsor: Institute for Government and Public Affairs

Field Label:
The purpose of this study is to gather important information from parents and guardians about their experiences caring for children. We are also interested in learning about the social and environmental factors that contribute to the health and wellbeing of children across Illinois. You are being asked to participate in this research because you have indicated to us that you are a parent or guardian to children under the age of 18 and you live in the state of Illinois. Your participation in this research is voluntary. Upon completion of the study, you will receive compensation in the amount that you have agreed to with the platform through which you entered this survey.

We would like to ask you to complete our online questionnaire. At the end of the questionnaire, you will have the opportunity to share your email address if you would like to receive a report of our survey findings or be contacted about future research opportunities. The primary risk to you is a loss of confidentiality. If you choose to share your contact information, we will take steps to protect the data you provide to us to minimize this risk. You may also feel uncomfortable answering some of the questions about your experiences as a parent. There are no direct benefits to you for participating in this research. The information we learn from you and others like you will potentially inform future policy and public health initiatives to improve the lives of children across the state.

We will store your information in secure computer systems available only to authorized study staff. Information that identifies you will be stored in computer systems designed for this type of information. Data stored outside of these specific systems will not include information that can identify you directly. De-identified data from this study will be shared publicly via a data dashboard on our study website so that others may use these data to answer their own research questions about children's health and well-being. We will provide the data in a format that allows other researchers to connect your data with other data sets. This will be done by providing the researchers with the ZIP code or county in which you live. We will remove or code all other personal information that could directly identify you before the study data are shared. Despite these measures, we cannot guarantee the anonymity of your personal data.

If you have questions, complaints or concerns about this study, you can contact Dr. Sheena Martenies at smarte4@illinois.edu or 217-300-9375.

If you have any questions about your rights as a research subject, including concerns, complaints, or to offer input, you may call the Office for the Protection of Research Subjects (OPRS) at 217-333-2670 or e-mail OPRS at irb@illinois.edu.

It should take 30 minutes to complete the questionnaire. Participation in this study is voluntary. You can choose not to take part. You can choose not to finish the questionnaire or omit any question you prefer not to answer without penalty or loss of benefits. 

By continuing the survey, you are verifying that you are age 18 or older and are giving your consent to participate.

Thank you for your participation in the IL Childcare and Children's Health Study!

Do you wish to continue with the survey?
